title: 5G Future Forum completes first-ever transatlantic real-time 5G holographic meeting using MATSUKO
text: LAS VEGAS, Sept. 25, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The 5G Future Forum ( 5GFF ) and MATSUKO will lead the first-ever real-time transatlantic holographic collaborative meeting, connecting multiple people as holograms. The holograms will be connected from New York in the U.S. using Verizon's 5G network; Toronto, Canada, using Bell Canada's 5G network and from London, UK using Vodafone's 5G network. The first-of-its-kind demonstration will be available to see at Mobile World Congress Las Vegas in Booth #1533, located in GSMA's Open Gateway Zone.

title: As Hurricane Lee looms in the Atlantic, Verizon is ready
text: AUGUSTA, Maine, Sept. 14, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- With Lee lurking in the Atlantic, Verizon's team is working to ensure its people and assets are ready to be deployed at a moment's notice to assist with rapid recovery efforts of critical communications infrastructure. With the potential for flooding and power loss along the northeastern sea wall and specifically in northeastern Maine, Verizon's network team is preparing for potential impacts. While the use of battery and generator backup systems in critical locations such as cell sites and network operations centers serve to minimize the risk of disruption if commercial power is lost, even the most hardened infrastructure can be shaken by Mother Nature.

title: Verizon waives call and text charges to Libya and in-country roaming charges
text: NEW YORK, Sept. 13, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- In response to the devastation caused by the catastrophic floods in Libya, Verizon is making it easier for customers in the United States to communicate with loved ones overseas who have been impacted by the disaster. Effective September 11 through September 24, Verizon will waive international long distance charges for calls and texts from its postpaid consumer and business wireless customers, as well as residential landline customers, to Libya. All calls will be rated at $0.00 per minute. In addition, Verizon will waive international roaming and data charges for customers traveling in Libya. For prepaid customers using Straight Talk, Tracfone, Total by Verizon, Simple Mobile, Safelink, Walmart Family Mobile, Verizon Prepaid, Net10, GoSmart, and Page Plus, Verizon will waive international long distance charges on calls to Libya from September 14 through September 24. Libya has also been added to the international calling destinations included with Visible+.

title: Verizon waives call and text charges to Morocco and in-country roaming charges
text: NEW YORK, Sept. 12, 2023 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- In response to the devastating earthquake in Morocco, Verizon is helping its customers in the United States connect with their loved ones overseas who have been impacted by the disaster. Effective September 11 through September 24, Verizon will waive international long distance charges for calls and texts from its postpaid consumer customers, as well as residential landline customers, to Morocco. All calls will be rated at $0.00 per minute. In addition, Verizon will waive international roaming and data charges for customers traveling in Morocco. For prepaid and TracFone customers, Verizon will waive international long distance charges on calls to Morocco.
